title: conohoria racemosa
intro: Conohoria racemosa, also known as Mountain Conewong or Wild Apple, is a species of flowering plant in the family Rutaceae. The plant is native to eastern Australia and has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues, coughs, and colds. The medicinal properties of Conohoria racemosa are attributed to its antiseptic, antibacterial, and anti-inflammatory properties, which contribute to its health benefits, including reducing fever and relieving congestion. Conohoria racemosa can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to harness its medicinal properties and provide relief from various health issues.
